mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains below that level for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following trigger activation are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) above its 3-month moving average signals strengthening labor demand and economic momentum, driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations rise and recession risk declines.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and forward earnings risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below its 6-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and margin compression, producing negative equity returns over 20 trading days as profit expectations deteriorate and cost pressures persist.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 0.5 index points
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO decline and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or negative INDPRO signals fail to predict negative returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and forward earnings risk, triggering negative equity returns over 60 trading days as investors reduce growth forecasts and increase equity risk premiums.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.0% and remains above that level for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between UNRATE and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or elevated UNRATE fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3.5% combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 3.0%) signals restrictive monetary policy that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real cash flow growth, producing negative 20-day returns.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting 20-day returns, or joint trigger fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors increase equity allocations and earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth fails to predict positive returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1.3 million units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat signals demand exhaustion and forward consumption weakness, producing negative 5-day equity returns as investors reprrice growth and recession probabilities upward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1.3 million units AND RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.2%
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient between HOUST and RSAFS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or joint trigger fails to predict negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances.
